volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 3.1.458b - Section 4.3.552)

sections.label: Section 3.1.458b
sections.original: Quid id refert tua ,
sections.translation: What matters that to you , so long as you get the money ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.459_460a
sections.original: Qui monet quasi adiuvat .
sections.translation: He who advises is as good as an accomplice . Do you follow .

sections.label: Section 3.1.461b
sections.original: Leno , cave in té sit mora mihi .—
sections.translation: Procurer , take care that you don ’ t cause me any delay .

sections.label: Section 4.1.462
sections.original: Edepol nugatorem lepidum lepide hunc nactust Phaedromus .
sections.translation: By my faith , Phaedromus has cleverly met with this clever rogue ; but whether a salt-water rogue [*] or a dry-land one I ’ m the rather to say he is , I really don ’ t know . The costume that I ’ ve lent I fear I shan ’ t get back .

sections.label: Section 4.1.465
sections.original: quamquam cum istoc mihi negoti nihil est : ipsi Phaedromo
sections.translation: Although I have nothing whatever to do with him ( I trusted Phaedromus himself ), still I ’ ll keep an eye upon him . But until he comes out of doors , I ’ ll point out in what place you may easily meet with each person , that he mayn ’ t lose his labour through too much trouble , if any one wishes to meet either a rascal or one without rascality , or an honest man or a dishonest one .

sections.label: Section 4.1.470
sections.original: qui periurum convenire volt hominem ito in comitium ;
sections.translation: He who desires to meet with a perjured fellow , let him go into the courts of law [*] ; he who wants a liar and a braggart , near the rites of Cloacina [*] . The rich and erring husbands seek you at the magisterial halls of the Basilica . There , too , will be the worn-out harlots , and those who are wont to haggle for them . Contributors to pic-nic dinners [*] you ’ ll find in the fishmarket .

sections.label: Section 4.1.475
sections.original: in foro infimo boni homines atque dites ambulant ,
sections.translation: In the lower part of the Forum good men [*] and opulent do walk ; in the middle , near the canal [*] , there are the mere puffers-off . Beyond the lake [*] of Curtius are impudent , talkative , and malevolent fellows , who boldly , without reason , utter calumnies about another , and who , themselves , have sufficient that might with truth be said against them .

sections.label: Section 4.1.480
sections.original: sub veteribus , ibi sunt qui dant quique accipiunt faenore .
sections.translation: There , at the old shops [*] , are these who lend and those who borrow at interest . Behind the Temple of Castor there are those to whom unguardedly you may be lending to your cost . There , in the Etrurian street , are those men who hold themselves [*] on sale . In the Velabrum [*] you ’ ll find either baker , or butcher , or soothsayer ; either those who sell retail themselves , or supply to others things to be sold by retail .

sections.label: Section 4.1.485
sections.original: ditis damnosos maritos apud Leucadiam Oppiam .
sections.translation: Rich sinning husbands you ’ ll find at the house of Oppian Leucadia [*] . But , meantime , the door makes a noise ; I must curb my tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.2.487
sections.original: I tú prae , virgo : nón queo quod póne me est serváre .
sections.translation: Maiden , do you go before ; what is behind me I cannot keep my eye upon . Both the trinkets of gold , and all the clothing that she had , were his own , he said .

sections.label: Section 4.2.489
sections.original: Nemo it infitias .
sections.translation: No one is going to deny it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.489b
sections.original: At tamen meliusculum est monere .
sections.translation: Still , however , it ’ s somewhat better for me to remind you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.490
sections.original: Memento promisisse te , si quisquam hanc liberali
sections.translation: Remember that you ’ ve undertaken , that , if any one should assert in course of law that she ’ s properly free , all the money is to be returned to me-thirty minae .

sections.label: Section 4.2.492b
sections.original: Meminero , de istoc quietus esto .
sections.translation: I shall remember ; be easy about that ; and now I say the same .

sections.label: Section 4.2.493b
sections.original: Et quidem meminisse ego haec volam te .
sections.translation: But I wish you to remember this well .

sections.label: Section 4.2.494
sections.original: Memini , et mancupio tibi dabo .
sections.translation: I remember , and I shall deliver her to you on warranty [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.2.494b
sections.original: Egon ab lenone quicquam
sections.translation: And am I to take anything on warranty from a Procurer , people who have nothing of their own except a tongue only ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.496
sections.original: qui abiurant si quid creditum est ? alienos mancupatis ,
sections.translation: who , if anything ’ s entrusted them , deny it upon oath ? You Procurers dispose of what belongs to others , you give liberty to what belongs to others , and what belongs to others your give your commands to ; no guarantee of ownership is there in the transfer to yourselves , nor are you yourselves guarantees to another person . The race of Procurers , among mortals , in my way of thinking at least ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.500
sections.original: ut muscae , culices , cimices pedesque pulicesque :
sections.translation: are just like flies , gnats , bugs , lice , and fleas — a plague , a mischief , and a nuisance ; you are of no serviceable use , and no respectable person dares to stand with you in the Forum ; he that does associate with you , they censure him , he ’ s spat upon and abused ; they say that he has lost his property and his honor , although he has done nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 4.2.505
sections.original: Edepol lenones meo animo novisti , lusce , lepide .
sections.translation: Upon my faith , my clever one-eyed friend , in my way of thinking , you are well acquainted with the Procurers .

sections.label: Section 4.2.506
sections.original: Eodem hércle vos pono et paro : parissimi estis hibus :
sections.translation: You bankers , i ’ faith , I put and place in the same rank ; you are the very counterparts of them . They , at least , are on sale in dark corners , you in the very Forum [*] . You tear men to pieces with usury , they by persuading them amiss and by means of their dens . Full many a proposed statute [*] has the public confirmed on your account ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.510
sections.original: quas vos rogatas rumpitis : aliquam reperitis rimam ;
sections.translation: which when confirmed you break ; some loophole you find out ; just as boiling water becomes cold , so do you deem the laws .

sections.label: Section 4.2.512
sections.original: Tacuisse mavellem .
sections.translation: I ’ d rather I ’ d held my tongue .

sections.label: Section 4.2.512b
sections.original: Hau male meditate maledicax es .
sections.translation: Not wrongfully , and with good reason , are you abusive against them .

sections.label: Section 4.2.513
sections.original: Indignis si male dicitur , male dictum id esse dico ,
sections.translation: If abuse is uttered against those who deserve it not , that I do hold to be abuse ; but if it is uttered against those who are deserving , it is fair censure , in my way of thinking , at least .

sections.label: Section 4.2.515
sections.original: ego mancupem te nil moror nec lenonem alium quemquam .
sections.translation: I care nothing about your warranty , nor about any other Procurer whatever . Lyco , do you want anything with me ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.516b
sections.original: Bene vale .
sections.translation: Heartily fare you well .

sections.label: Section 4.2.516c
sections.original: Vale .
sections.translation: Farewell . ( Going .)

sections.label: Section 4.2.516d
sections.original: Heus tu , tibi ego dico .
sections.translation: Hark you ! I say to you —

sections.label: Section 4.2.517
sections.original: Eloquere , quid vis ?
sections.translation: Say on ; what do you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.517b
sections.original: Quaeso , ut hanc cures , ut bene sit isti .
sections.translation: Prithee do you take care that all ’ s well with her ( pointing to PLANESIUM ) ; I ’ ve brought her up in my house carefully and in chastity .

sections.label: Section 4.2.518b
sections.original: Si huius miseret ,
sections.translation: If you have such compassion for her , pray , what would you give for it to be all well with her ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.519b
sections.original: Malúm .
sections.translation: A plaguy mishap for yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.519c
sections.original: Opust hóc qui te procures .
sections.translation: You need take due care on that score about yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.520
sections.original: Quid stulta ploras ? ne time , bene hercle vendidi égo te ;
sections.translation: Why , simpleton , do you cry ? Don ’ t be afraid ; upon my faith , I ’ ve sold you favourably . Take care , will you , and be a good girl ; now prettily accompany him , pretty one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.522
sections.original: Summane , numquid nunciam me vis ?
sections.translation: Summanus , do you want anything with me at present ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.522b
sections.original: Vale atque salve ,
sections.translation: Fare you well , and health attend you . ( Aside .) For you ’ ve kindly given me your services and your money .

sections.label: Section 4.2.524
sections.original: Salutem multam dicito patrono .
sections.translation: Give abundant greetings to my patron .

sections.label: Section 4.2.524b
sections.original: Nuntiabo .—
sections.translation: I ’ ll give them .

sections.label: Section 4.2.525
sections.original: Numquid vis , leno ?
sections.translation: Procurer , do you wish for anything ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.525b
sections.original: Istas minas decem , qui me procurem ,
sections.translation: Give me those ten minae for me to manage for myself with , until things are better with me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.526b
sections.original: Dabuntur , cras peti iubeto .—
sections.translation: They shall be paid ; order them to be fetched to-morrow .

sections.label: Section 4.2.527
sections.original: Quando bene gessi rem , volo hic in fano supplicare .
sections.translation: Since I ’ ve successfully finished the matter , I wish to return thanks here in the Temple . For long since , when a little girl , I bought her for ten minae ; but him who sold her to me , never since then have I set eyes upon .

sections.label: Section 4.2.530
sections.original: periisse credo . quíd id mea refert ? ego argentum habeo .
sections.translation: I think he ’ s dead . What matters that to me ? I ’ ve got the money . The man to whom the Gods are propitious , for him , no doubt , they throw gain in his way . Now will I give my attention to my devotions ; it ’ s clear that he has a kind regard for me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.533
sections.original: Nón ego nunc medíocri incedo irátus iracúndia ,
sections.translation: I ’ m come now , inflamed with wrath in no moderate degree , but with that same with which I ’ ve learned to deal destruction upon cities .

sections.label: Section 4.3.535
sections.original: nunc nisi tu mihi propere properas dare iam triginta minas ,
sections.translation: At once now , unless this moment you make haste instantly to pay me the thirty minae which I left with you , make haste to lay down your life .

sections.label: Section 4.3.537
sections.original: Non edepol nunc ego te mediocri macto infortunio ,
sections.translation: By my troth , to no little mischief do I now devote you , but to that same to which I am wont to devote that man to whom I owe nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 4.3.539
sections.original: Ne te mi facias ferocem aut supplicare censeas .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be making yourself bold with me , or suppose that I shall be entreating you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.540
sections.original: Nec tu me quidem umquam subiges , redditum ut reddam tibi ,
sections.translation: And you , indeed , shall never force me to pay you what has been paid , nor shall I give it you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.541b
sections.original: Idem ego istuc quom credebam credidi ,
sections.translation: I thought this , when I entrusted you with it , that you would repay nothing at all .

sections.label: Section 4.3.542b
sections.original: Quor nunc a me igitur petis ?
sections.translation: Why then are you now asking it back of me ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.543
sections.original: Scire volo quoi reddidisti .
sections.translation: I wish to know to whom you have paid it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.543b
sections.original: Lusco liberto tuo ,
sections.translation: To your one-eyed freed-man ; he said that he was called Summanus ; I paid it to him , who brought me this sealed letter .

sections.label: Section 4.3.545b
sections.original: Quas tu mihi tabellas ?
sections.translation: What letter of mine , what one-eyed freed-man , what people called Summanus are you dreaming about ? I really have no freed-man at all .

sections.label: Section 4.3.547b
sections.original: Fácis sapientius
sections.translation: You act more wisely than a portion of the military men who have freed-men , and then forsake them .

sections.label: Section 4.3.549
sections.original: Quid feci †
sections.translation: What have you done ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.549b
sections.original: Quod mandasti feci , túi honoris gratia ,
sections.translation: What you requested me , I ’ ve done for your sake ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.550
sections.original: tuom qui signum ad me attulisset , nuntium ne spernerem .
sections.translation: that I mightn ’ t slight the messenger who had brought your seal ’ s impression .

sections.label: Section 4.3.551
sections.original: Stultior stulto fuisti , qui tabellis crederes .
sections.translation: More fool than fool were you to give credit to this letter .

sections.label: Section 4.3.552
sections.original: Quis res publica et privata geritur , nonne eis crederem ?
sections.translation: To that by which matters both public and private are carried on ought I not to have given credit ? I ’ ll be off ; the money has been properly paid you . Warrior , farewell .
